volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Braggart Soldier (Section 4.5.1203b - Section 4.6.1257)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Miles Gloriosus, or The Braggart Captain

sections.label: Section 4.5.1203b
sections.original: Vt multa verba feci , ut lenta materies fuit .
sections.translation: How many words she did utter ! How the matter was protracted ! But in the end I obtained what I wanted , and I granted her

sections.label: Section 4.5.1205
sections.original: quae voluit , quae postulavit ; te quoque ei dono dedi .
sections.translation: what she wanted and what she asked of me . I made a present of you also to her .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1206
sections.original: Etiam me ? quo modo ego vivam sine te ?
sections.translation: What — me , too ? In what way shall I exist without you ?

sections.label: Section 4.5.1206b
sections.translation: Come , be of good heart ; I ’ ll make you free from exit , too . But I used all endeavours , if I could by any method persuade her to go away , and not take you with her , she forced me , however .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1209b
sections.translation: In the Gods and yourself I ’ ll place my trust . Yet , at the last ,

sections.label: Section 4.5.1210
sections.original: etsi istuc mi acerbumst , quia ero te carendum est optimo ,
sections.translation: although it is bitter to me that I must be deprived of an excellent master , yourself , at least it is a pleasure to me that , through my means , by reason of the excellence of your beauty , this has happened to you with regard to this lady neighbour , whom I am now introducing to you .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1213
sections.original: Quid opust verbis ? libertatem tibi ego et divitias dabo ,
sections.translation: What need of words ? I ’ ll give you liberty and wealth if you obtain her for me .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1214b
sections.translation: I ’ ll win her .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1214c
sections.translation: But I ’ m impatient .

sections.label: Section 4.5.1214d
sections.translation: But moderation is requisite ;

sections.label: Section 4.5.1215
sections.original: moderare animo , ne sis cupidus . séd eccam ipsam , egreditur foras .
sections.translation: curb your desires ; don ’ t be over anxious . But see , here she is herself ; she is coming out of doors .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1216
sections.translation: Mistress , see ! the Captain ’ s near .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1216b
sections.translation: Where is he ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1216c
sections.translation: Only look to the left .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1217
sections.original: Aspicito limis oculis , ne ille nos se sentiat videre .
sections.translation: Eye him askance , that he mayn ’ t perceive that we are looking at him .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1218
sections.original: Video . edepol nunc nos tempus est malas peioris fieri .
sections.translation: I see him . Troth , now ’ s the time , in our mischief , for us to become supremely mischievous .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1219
sections.translation: ’ Tis for you to begin .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1219b
sections.translation: Prithee , did you see him yourself ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1220
sections.translation: ( Aside .) don ’ t spare your voice , so that he may hear .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1220b
sections.translation: By my troth , I talked with his own self , at my ease , as long as I pleased , at my leisure , at my own discretion , just as I wished .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1222
sections.translation: Do you hear what she says ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1222b
sections.original: Audio . quam laeta est , quia ted adiit .
sections.translation: I hear . How delighted she is because she had access to you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1223
sections.translation: O happy woman that you are !

sections.label: Section 4.6.1223b
sections.translation: How I do seem to be loved !

sections.label: Section 4.6.1223c
sections.translation: You are deserving of it .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1224
sections.original: Permirum ecastor praedicas , te adiisse atque exorasse ;
sections.translation: By my troth , ’ tis passing strange what you say , that you had access to him and prevailed . They say that he is usually addressed , like a king , through letters or messengers .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1226
sections.original: Namque edepol vix fuit cópia adeundi atque impetrandi .
sections.translation: But , i ’ faith , ’ twas with difficulty I had an opportunity of approaching and beseeching him .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1227
sections.original: Vt tu inclitus esinclitu's apud múlieres .
sections.translation: How renowned you are among the fair .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1227b
sections.translation: I shall submit , since Venus wills it so .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1228
sections.original: Veneri pol habeo gratiam , eandémque et oro et quaeso ,
sections.translation: By heavens ! I return to Venus grateful thanks , and her I do beseech and entreat , that I may win him whom I love and whom I seek to win ,

sections.label: Section 4.6.1230
sections.original: benignusque erga me ut siet , quod cupiam ne gravetur .
sections.translation: and that to me he may prove gentle , and not make a difficulty about what I desire .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1231
sections.original: Spero ita futurum , quamquam illum multae sibi expetessunt :
sections.translation: I hope it may be so ; although many ladies are seeking to win him for themselves , he disdains them and estranges himself from all but you alone .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1233
sections.original: Ergo iste metus me macerat , quod ille fastidiosust ,
sections.translation: Therefore this fear torments me , since he is so disdainful , lest his eyes , when he beholds me , should change his sentiments ,

sections.label: Section 4.6.1235
sections.original: atque eius elegantia meam extémplo speciem spernat .
sections.translation: and his own gracefulness should at once disdain my form .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1236
sections.translation: He will not do so ; be of good heart .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1236b
sections.translation: How she does slight herself !

sections.label: Section 4.6.1237
sections.original: Metuo , ne praedicatio tua nunc meam formam exsuperet .
sections.translation: I fear lest your account may have surpassed my looks .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1238
sections.original: Istuc curavi , ut opínione illius pulchrior sis .
sections.translation: I ’ ve taken care of this , that you shall be fairer than his expectations .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1239
sections.original: Si pol me nolet ducere uxórem , genua amplectar
sections.translation: Troth , if he shall refuse to take me as his wife , by heavens I ’ ll embrace his knees

sections.label: Section 4.6.1240
sections.original: atque obsecrabo ; alio modo , si non quibo impetrare ,
sections.translation: and entreat him ! If I shall be unable to prevail on him , in some way or other , I ’ ll put myself to death . I ’ m quite sure that without him I cannot live .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1242
sections.translation: I see that I must prevent this woman ’ s death . Shall I accost her ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1242b
sections.translation: By no means ; for you will be making yourself cheap if you lavish yourself away of your own accord . Let her come spontaneously , seek you , court you , strive to win you .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1245
sections.original: sine : perdere istam gloriam vis , quám habes ? cave sis faxis .
sections.translation: Unless you wish to lose that glory which you have , please have a care what you do . For I know that this was never the lot of any mortal , except two persons , yourself and Phaon of Lesbos [*] , to be loved so desperately .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1248
sections.original: Eo íntro , aut tu illum huc evoca foras , mea Milphidippa .
sections.translation: I ’ ll go in-doors [*] — or , my dear Milphidippa , do you call him out of doors .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1249
sections.translation: Aye ; let ’ s wait until some one comes out .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1249b
sections.translation: I can ’ t restrain myself

sections.label: Section 4.6.1250
sections.translation: from going in to him .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1250b
sections.translation: The door ’ s fastened .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1250c
sections.translation: I ’ ll break it in then .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1250d
sections.translation: You are not in your senses .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1251
sections.original: Si amavit umquam aut si parem sapientiam habet ac formam ,
sections.translation: If he has ever loved , or if he has wisdom equal to his beauty , whatever I may do through love , he will pardon me by reason of his compassionate feelings .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1253
sections.original: Vt , quaeso , amore perditast tuo misera .
sections.translation: Prithee , do see , how distracted the poor thing is with love .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1253b
sections.translation: ’ Tis mutual in us .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1254
sections.translation: Hush ! don ’ t you let her hear .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1254b
sections.original: Quid astitisti obstupida ? cur non pultas ?
sections.translation: Why do you stand stupefied ? Why don ’ t you knock ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1255
sections.original: Quia non est intus quem ego volo .
sections.translation: Because he is not within whom I want .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1255b
sections.translation: How do you know [*] ?

sections.label: Section 4.6.1255c
sections.translation: By my troth , I do know it easily ; for my nose would scent him if he were within .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1256b
sections.translation: She is a diviner .

sections.label: Section 4.6.1257
sections.original: Quia mé amat , propterea Venus fecit eam ut divinaret .
sections.translation: Because she is in love with me , Venus has made her prophesy .
